title: Support `resetall
body:
Hello!

I ran into timescale issues with simulating Alex Forencich's IPs. and realized the issue is that `` `resetall `` seems to not be fully supported.

alexforencich/verilog-axi rtl/axi_dma.v: <https://github.com/alexforencich/verilog-axi/blob/38915fb5330cb8270b454afc0140a94489dc56db/rtl/axi_dma.v#L27-L29>

I believe the Verilator implementation only resets `` `default_nettype ``:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/bb871728c92553c3813f556782807b5879135ea1/src/verilog.l#L1065-L1066

However, these are the requirements from the LRM:

> ### 22.3 `` `resetall ``
> When the `` `resetall `` compiler directive is encountered during compilation, all compiler directives are set to the default values. This is useful for ensuring that only directives that are desired in compiling a particular source file are active.

The affected directives seem to be the following:

* `` `default_nettype `` [22.8]: `` `resetall `` resets to `wire`
* `` `celldefine `` [22.10]: `` `resetall `` acts as `` `endcelldefine ``
* `` `pragma `` [22.11.1]: `` `resetall `` reverts all pragmas to defaults
* `` `timescale ``[22.7]: `` `resetall `` reverts timescale back to tool default
* `` `unconnected_drive `` [22.9]: `` `resetall `` acts as `` `nounconnected_drive ``

Here is the Icarus implementation: <https://github.com/steveicarus/iverilog/blob/30123f89451939fc946a2395c91e39911fcd354a/lexor.lex#L1616-L1622>

I can add a fix this weekend. Would this be best fixed by adding more to verilog.l? Or is there a good .cpp file to put a resetall function?

Thanks!
